# taipan_wardryx_policy (Resource)

Manages one Wardryx admin policy-as-code document (PUT/GET/DELETE /v1/policies/{id}), layered on top of Wardryx's own -policy/WARDRYX_POLICY file-loaded rules, which this resource never touches -- those stay a permanent floor no API write can remove. Requires the provider's wardryx_key to be an admin-role Wardryx bearer key; a non-admin key fails with a 403 diagnostic from the Wardryx API.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `id` (String) The policy id, e.g. ops-guard. Wardryx addresses policies by this id, not by name -- there is no rename, only PUT-by-id and DELETE-by-id, so changing it forces a new resource.
- `target` (String) The agent:// glob this policy applies to ("*" matches any run of characters including "/"; "?" matches exactly one character).

### Optional

- `allow_domains` (List of String) Network destinations a matched agent's declared tools may reach. Only restricts domains a DecideRequest actually declares; see Wardryx's own README for the exact semantics.
- `deny_above_usd` (Number) Hard, non-approvable cost ceiling: exceeding it denies outright, and no approval_token -- however validly minted -- can turn that deny into an allow. Zero/unset means no hard ceiling.
- `deny_if_unattested` (Boolean) Denies any request from an agent with no live attestation (attestation method "" or "none").
- `deny_tool` (List of String) Tool names this policy refuses outright.
- `max_steps` (Number) Caps how many steps a run may take. Zero/unset means no cap.
- `name` (String) Human-readable policy name, used in Wardryx's Reason strings and logs. Defaults to target (server-side, in Wardryx's decision engine) when left empty.
- `require_human_above_usd` (Number) Estimated-cost threshold above which a human must approve the action (a hold, not a hard deny). Zero/unset means no threshold.

### Read-Only

- `updated_at` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp of this policy's last write, as reported by Wardryx.
